title: Cedar Raised Planter with Storage Shelf
summary: A waist-height cedar picket planter on a 2x4 frame with a storage shelf built in below the soil box — unlike this catalog's other footed cedar planters, which have no under-box shelf.
description:
This planter adds something this catalog's other elevated cedar planters don't have: a genuine storage shelf built into the frame below the soil box, not just a slatted false bottom for drainage. Build two 2x4 leg frames first, then join them with the longer 2x4 cross rails on a level surface — that frame is what carries both the planter box above and the shelf below, so getting it square here matters twice as much as on a planter with no lower shelf.

Attach the long cedar pickets to the legs with the shorter screws, and if there's extra room left over, center any gap deliberately — cedar pickets expand with moisture, and a small built-in expansion gap prevents the panel from binding as the wood swells. The end pieces go on next, screwed at a slight angle into the legs for a stronger bite than a straight-in screw gives at this joint.

The bottom shelf and the planter's own soil floor are built the same way — picket boards screwed to the frame with the shorter screws — but they serve two different jobs at two different heights: one holds soil, one holds pots, tools, or bags of mulch. Line the inside of the planter box with landscaping fabric to contain the soil, and cut a few drainage holes in the fabric if it's a watertight liner rather than a porous one.

materials.name: Cedar fence pickets, 5-1/2 in x 6 ft

cutList.material: Cedar fence pickets, 5-1/2 in x 6 ft
Cedar fence pickets, 5-1/2 in x 6 ft
Cedar fence pickets, 5-1/2 in x 6 ft

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut four legs at 30-1/2", four end frame rails at 21" and four side frame rails at 45" from the 2x4, 8 ft or stud length. Cut four side pickets at 46-3/4", ten end and shelf pickets at 21" and six inside bottom pickets at 20-3/8" from the Cedar fence pickets, 5-1/2 in x 6 ft. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to five 8 ft 2x4, 8 ft or stud length and eight 6 ft Cedar fence pickets, 5-1/2 in x 6 ft. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.
materials: Cedar fence pickets, 5-1/2 in x 6 ft

steps.title: Build the leg frames
steps.body: Build two leg frames using the longer 2-1/2" screws, two screws per joint and glue.

steps.title: Join the leg frames
steps.body: Attach the two leg frames together with the long side frame rails.

steps.title: Attach the side pickets
steps.body: Use the shorter 1-5/8" screws to attach the long cedar pickets to the legs. If there's extra space, center any gap in the middle to allow for moisture expansion.
materials: Cedar fence pickets, 5-1/2 in x 6 ft

steps.title: Add the end pieces
steps.body: Add the end pickets, screwing at a slight angle into the 2x4 legs.
materials: Cedar fence pickets, 5-1/2 in x 6 ft

steps.title: Attach the tops
steps.body: Attach the top rails with the longer 2-1/2" screws.

steps.title: Add the shelf and planter floor, then finish
steps.body: Add the shelf boards to the bottom storage shelf and attach with the shorter screws. Repeat for the planter's own soil floor. Line the inside of the planter with landscaping fabric, cutting drainage holes if the fabric is watertight.
materials: Cedar fence pickets, 5-1/2 in x 6 ft

unresolvedImages.alt: Cedar raised planter box with a storage shelf built in below
